items.name: Handala (Iran-linked hacktivists)
items.targets: Stryker medical device manufacturer (US) - wiped 200K devices causing global network disruption; Raz Zimmt (head of Iran Desk at Israeli security institutes)
items.ttps: T1485 Data Destruction (wiper attacks), T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1498 Network Denial of Service, supply chain targeting of medical infrastructure

items.name: Iran MOIS (Ministry of Intelligence and Security)
items.targets: Multiple US and Middle East targets, colluding with criminal groups for cyberattacks during heightened Iran conflict
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing, T1078 Valid Accounts, collaboration with cybercriminal infrastructure

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Syed Professional Services, TDS Construction Group, Silvon Software Group
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, double extortion

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Telus Digital (Canadian company) - claims 1 petabyte data theft
items.ttps: T1530 Data from Cloud Storage, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, large-scale data exfiltration

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: Referenced in context of nation-state attacks and exploitation campaigns
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing, T1203 Exploitation for Client Execution, supply chain attacks

items.name: CLOP Ransomware
items.targets: Referenced in podcast discussing $46M theft from US government
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1078 Valid Accounts, exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities

items.name: Phishing Operators (Multiple)
items.targets: Microsoft 365 users via OAuth Device Code abuse and Cloudflare anti-bot bypass techniques; Microsoft Copilot users via AI email summary manipulation
items.ttps: T1566.002 Spearphishing Link, T1528 Steal Application Access Token, T1550 Use Alternate Authentication Material (OAuth abuse), T1090 Proxy (Cloudflare abuse)

canada_landscape.summary: Two significant Canadian breaches reported in the last 48 hours: Loblaw (major retailer) suffered a data breach exposing sensitive customer information, and Telus Digital (Canadian telecommunications/BPO) confirmed a breach after ShinyHunters claimed theft of 1 petabyte of data. Canada cancelled hundreds of visas over Iran connections amid heightened geopolitical tensions. No CCCS advisories were identified in the feed. New Bill C-22 introduced for lawful access legislation to help track criminals online.
canada_landscape.retailers: Loblaw (major Canadian grocer/pharmacy chain) confirmed data breach exposing sensitive customer information
